findings.description: The metadata-driven strategy path is still incomplete end to end: `work-packages.yaml` defines no per-package `metadata.loc_estimate` / `metadata.alternatives_count` / `metadata.package_kind`, and no work package owns the required `work-packages.schema.json` change to allow those fields under strict validation. As written, T3's structured-metadata branch cannot be exercised or validated.
findings.resolution: Add the schema change to a concrete work package (likely `wp-schema`) and populate the relevant package metadata in `work-packages.yaml`, or relax the spec/tasks if inference-only selection is acceptable.

findings.description: The convergence-loop design still assumes dispatcher and synthesizer interfaces that do not exist: it passes unsupported `output_dir` / `timeout` arguments to `ReviewOrchestrator.dispatch_and_wait()`, feeds `ReviewResult.findings` directly into `VendorResult`, and reads `ConsensusReport` like a dict (`f["agreed_criticality"]`, `consensus.summary[...]`). Implementing the design literally will fail against the current `review_dispatcher.py` and `consensus_synthesizer.py` contracts.
findings.resolution: Revise the design and T2 to specify the real adapter flow: instantiate `ReviewOrchestrator`, convert each result's JSON payload into the `Finding` / `VendorResult` objects expected by `ConsensusSynthesizer`, then consume `ConsensusReport` via documented attributes or `to_dict()` before reading summary fields.

findings.description: `IMPL_FIX` still depends on a targeted `--include-vendor` dispatch path, but the current review dispatcher only supports broadcast execution with optional `--exclude-vendor`. The plan therefore has no implementable contract for routing a fix round back to the recorded lead vendor only.
findings.resolution: Either extend `review_dispatcher.py` / `ReviewOrchestrator` with an explicit include-target vendor capability and cover it in tasks/tests, or redesign `IMPL_FIX` to invoke a single-vendor adapter directly instead of relying on the existing dispatcher CLI.

findings.description: The executable convergence algorithm still does not encode the spec's `disagreement => ESCALATE` rule. In the current design, the blocking set only checks confirmed/unconfirmed medium+ findings, and T2/test acceptance never requires a disagreement path, so a round containing only disagreement findings can be treated as converged.
findings.resolution: Add an explicit check that any `disagreement` status (or `recommended_disposition == "escalate"`) transitions to `ESCALATE` before convergence is declared, and add unit coverage for that path in T2.
